children.name: ParseBuildRequest
children.purpose: Validate and parse the incoming build_request JSON to extract action and parameters
children.behavior: Check required fields based on action, return parsed action, repo, branch, config, build_id

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns parsed dict with action and required fields

children.name: CheckConcurrentBuild
children.purpose: Check if a build is already running for the given repo and branch
children.behavior: Query builds store for any build with same repo and branch and status 'running' or 'queued'
children.signature: def CheckConcurrentBuild(repo: str, branch: str) -> Optional[dict]

global_vars.description: Read builds to check for concurrent builds

data_operations.description: Query builds store for existing builds with same repo and branch

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns existing build record or None

children.name: CreateBuildRecord
children.purpose: Create a new build record in the builds store with initial status 'queued'
children.behavior: Generate unique build_id, set started_at, store repo, branch, config, empty logs
children.signature: def CreateBuildRecord(repo: str, branch: str, config: dict) -> str

global_vars.description: Write new build record to builds store

data_operations.description: Insert new build record with status 'queued'

acceptance_criteria.description: New build record created with status 'queued'

children.name: ExecuteBuildSteps
children.purpose: Run the build steps (compile, test, package) sequentially and update logs incrementally
children.behavior: For each step in config.build_steps, simulate execution, append log entries, update status to 'running' then 'completed' or 'failed'
children.signature: def ExecuteBuildSteps(build_id: str, config: dict) -> Tuple[str, list]

global_vars.description: Read build record to update status and logs

data_operations.description: Update build status and append logs

acceptance_criteria.description: Build status updated to 'completed' or 'failed' with logs

children.name: StoreArtifacts
children.purpose: Store build artifacts (e.g., packaged files) associated with the build
children.behavior: Create artifact records in artifacts store with build_id, path, size, created_at
children.signature: def StoreArtifacts(build_id: str, artifacts: list) -> list

global_vars.description: Write artifact records to artifacts store

data_operations.description: Insert artifact records linked to build_id

children.name: GetBuildStatus
children.purpose: Return current status and logs for a given build_id
children.behavior: Query builds store for build record, return status and logs

global_vars.description: Read build record to get status and logs

data_operations.description: Query builds store for specific build_id

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns status and logs for given build_id

children.name: ListBuilds
children.purpose: Return all build records filtered by optional repo, branch, status
children.behavior: Query builds store, apply filters, return list of build records
children.signature: def ListBuilds(repo: Optional[str] = None, branch: Optional[str] = None, status: Optional[str] = None) -> list

global_vars.description: Read builds store to list filtered records

data_operations.description: Query builds store with optional filters

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of build records matching filters

children.name: CancelBuild
children.purpose: Cancel a running build by updating its status to 'cancelled'
children.behavior: Check build status; if 'running' or 'queued', update to 'cancelled' and set finished_at; if already completed, no-op

global_vars.description: Read build record to check status, then write updated status

data_operations.description: Read build record to check current status
Update build status to 'cancelled' if applicable

constraints.description: Must not change status if build already completed

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns true if build was cancelled, false otherwise

children.name: FormatBuildResult
children.purpose: Assemble the final build_result dict from action-specific outputs
children.behavior: Combine success, build_id, status, logs into a single JSON response
children.signature: def FormatBuildResult(action: str, build_id: Optional[str], status: Optional[str], logs: Optional[list], success: bool) -> dict

governance_notes: Global state conservation verified: builds (read_write) covered by CheckConcurrentBuild (read), CreateBuildRecord (write), ExecuteBuildSteps (read_write), GetBuildStatus (read), ListBuilds (read), CancelBuild (read_write). artifacts (read_write) covered by StoreArtifacts (write). Note: artifacts read is not explicitly required by any child in this decomposition, but the parent requires read_write; if read is needed, a child must be added or an existing child must be extended. For now, only write is covered; read is missing. This is a governance gap that must be addressed.
